name: registry
description: The sport CATALOG. Owns the canonical Sport + Discipline + Position taxonomy that hardens the free-text `sport_key` + position identifier strings used across every Wave 3 sports module. Three entities. Sport (`spo_`) — top-level sport (`football`, `basketball`, `swimming`, `yoga`, `tennis`, `general_fitness`, 38 platform-seeded sports); every Team, AthleteEnrollment, Coach, Event, Session references `sport_key` which resolves to `Sport.slug`. Discipline (`dis_`) — sport sub-variant / competition format (`football_11v11`, `football_futsal`, `basketball_5v5`, `basketball_3x3`, `swimming_freestyle`, `swimming_relay`); one Sport hasMany Disciplines. Position (`pos_`) — sport-specific role / position (`football_goalkeeper`, `football_defender`, `football_midfielder`, `football_forward`; `basketball_point_guard`, `basketball_center`; `swimming_freestyler`; plus generic `coach`, `assistant_coach`, `medical_staff`); one Discipline hasMany Positions. Ships `data/platform-sports-catalog.json` — a 30+ sport seed loaded by SportCatalogSeeder on first-boot bootstrap. Platform-scoped (tenant_id NULL for platform-seeded rows); tenant customs (tenant_id NOT NULL) available only on Enterprise via the `sports_registry_custom` entitlement. Small + Medium tenants are locked to the platform catalog. NEVER carries `application_id` — the catalog crosses applications and every product deployment reads the same taxonomy. NEVER carries `region_id`, `branch_id`, `organization_id`, `scope_node_id`. Downstream sports modules reference this catalog via `sport_key` string lookup, not FK — so this module has `extendedBy: []` and every consumer stays loosely coupled. Wave 3a of the sports tier (priority 60 — boots after tenancy/application/entitlements, before every other sports module).

tenant.description: Tenant-facing read across the merged catalog (platform + tenant customs) + write on tenant customs only (Enterprise, via sports_registry_custom entitlement). Platform-seeded rows are strictly read-only on the tenant plane.

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant catalog administration. Platform admins can add/edit platform-level rows (visible to every tenant) + view every tenant custom + re-run the SportCatalogSeeder against the shipped JSON.
